description: Tasks rewritten to match actual generated data

tasks.instruction: Bookmark an early‑career individual research grant in the $50,000–$100,000 range whose application deadline falls after mid‑2025.
tasks.name: Bookmark an early‑career individual research grant in the $50,000–$100,000 range whose application deadline falls after mid‑2025.
tasks.description: Bookmark an early‑career individual research grant in the $50,000–$100,000 range whose application deadline falls after mid‑2025.

tasks.instruction: Within environmental research programs that fund under $500,000 per project, compare any two and add the one with the lower required institutional co‑funding percentage to your shortlist.
tasks.name: Within environmental research programs that fund under $500,000 per project, compare any two and add the one with the lower required institutional co‑funding percentage to your shortlist.
tasks.description: Within environmental research programs that fund under $500,000 per project, compare any two and add the one with the lower required institutional co‑funding percentage to your shortlist.

tasks.instruction: Start a new grant application for any funding call you have saved, set the project start date to January 1, 2026, choose a 24‑month duration, set the total requested budget to $95,000, and save the application as a draft.
tasks.name: Start a new grant application for any funding call you have saved, set the project start date to January 1, 2026, choose a 24‑month duration, set the total requested budget to $95,000, and save the application as a draft.
tasks.description: Start a new grant application for any funding call you have saved, set the project start date to January 1, 2026, choose a 24‑month duration, set the total requested budget to $95,000, and save the application as a draft.

tasks.instruction: In one of your draft applications, create three budget lines for personnel, equipment, and travel with an initial total over $100,000, then if the total exceeds $100,000 reduce the travel amount to $15,000, recalculate the totals, and save.
tasks.name: In one of your draft applications, create three budget lines for personnel, equipment, and travel with an initial total over $100,000, then if the total exceeds $100,000 reduce the travel amount to $15,000, recalculate the totals, and save.

tasks.instruction: In the help center’s eligibility section, bookmark the FAQ that explains who can act as the main project leader or Principal Investigator, then run a help search using a keyword phrase about PhD students being eligible as PI.

target_names: Who can be a Principal Investigator?

tasks.instruction: Create a funding email alert for national programs related to Artificial Intelligence in the ICT research area, delivered as a monthly summary and including opportunities with deadlines within the next 60 days.

tasks.instruction: In the funded projects database, filter for completed projects in a specific urban sustainability program with total funding over $200,000 that are led by universities, then select the first two matching projects and open their comparison view.

target_names: Smart Mobility Living Labs for Low-Carbon Districts

tasks.instruction: Find an online webinar focused on grant or application writing taking place in April 2026, register for it with your name, email, and institution, and submit the registration.

target_names: Grant Writing for First-Time Applicants (Online Webinar)

tasks.instruction: Locate a funding call for a climate‑focused data fellowship and use its contact form to send the program officer a message asking about the maximum allowed travel budget, stating that you are preparing an application and plan to include international conference travel.
